name: Pelvic Floor Physical Therapy
note: Specialized physical therapy for pelvic floor dysfunction, prolapse, incontinence, pelvic pain, vaginismus, and postpartum recovery. Prenatal and postpartum applications.

name: Myofascial Release
note: Hands-on manual therapy targeting myofascial restrictions causing pain, limited range of motion, and referred pain patterns throughout the body.

name: Sports Physical Therapy
note: Injury rehabilitation and performance optimization for athletes and active individuals. Includes movement assessment, corrective exercise, and return-to-sport protocols.

name: Vestibular Therapy
note: Specialized physical therapy for dizziness, vertigo, BPPV, vestibular neuritis, and balance disorders. Includes canalith repositioning maneuvers and gaze stabilization exercises.

name: TMJ Physical Therapy
note: Targeted therapy for temporomandibular joint dysfunction, jaw pain, clicking, headaches, and facial pain. Often paired with dental or oral appliance treatment.

name: Aquatic Therapy
note: Physical therapy performed in a warm water pool. Buoyancy reduces joint load, enabling movement that would be painful on land. Used for arthritis, fibromyalgia, and post-surgical rehab.

name: Occupational Therapy
note: Therapy focused on improving ability to perform daily activities after injury, illness, or developmental challenges. Includes sensory integration, upper extremity rehab, and cognitive rehabilitation.

name: Speech Therapy
note: Treatment for communication disorders, voice problems, swallowing difficulties (dysphagia), stuttering, and language processing. Also used for cognitive-communication deficits.

name: Lymphatic Drainage Massage
note: Gentle specialized massage technique that stimulates lymphatic circulation. Used for lymphedema, post-surgical swelling, detoxification support, and immune function.

name: Massage Therapy
note: General therapeutic massage for stress reduction, muscle tension relief, circulation, and pain management. Evidence supports benefit for anxiety, chronic pain, and blood pressure.

name: Deep Tissue Massage
note: Firm-pressure massage targeting deeper muscle layers and connective tissue. Used for chronic muscle tension, postural issues, and injury recovery. Soreness normal for 24-48 hours after.

name: Craniosacral Therapy
note: Gentle manual therapy working with the craniosacral rhythm (cerebrospinal fluid movement). Used for headaches, migraines, TMJ, trauma, and nervous system dysregulation.

name: Rolfing
note: Deep connective tissue bodywork using a 10-session series to realign fascia around gravity. Addresses chronic postural patterns, pain, and movement efficiency.

name: Reflexology
note: Applies targeted pressure to specific reflex points on feet, hands, or ears corresponding to organs and body systems. Used for relaxation, stress reduction, and complementary support.

name: Cupping Therapy
note: Creates suction on the skin to increase blood flow, decompress fascia, and reduce pain. Temporary skin discoloration (marks) are normal. Used in TCM and sports medicine.

name: Gua Sha
note: Instrument-assisted skin scraping technique that increases circulation and breaks up fascial adhesions. Used for muscle tension, neck pain, and lymphatic stimulation. Temporary redness is normal.

name: Trigger Point Therapy
note: Sustained pressure applied directly to hyperirritable trigger points in muscle tissue to release tension and referred pain patterns. Often integrated into massage or PT.

name: Chiropractic Adjustment
note: Controlled spinal and joint manipulations to improve joint mobility and reduce pain. Evidence-based for low back pain, neck pain, and headaches. Includes soft tissue techniques.

name: Osteopathic Manipulation
note: Full-body manual medicine by an osteopathic physician. Uses multiple techniques including HVLA, muscle energy, strain-counterstrain, and craniosacral. Integrates structural and systemic medicine.

name: Acupuncture
note: Insertion of fine needles at specific points along meridians to regulate qi and stimulate physiological responses. Evidence-based for pain, nausea, headache, and infertility support.

name: Acupressure
note: Applies firm finger or tool pressure to acupuncture points without needles. Self-administered or practitioner-delivered. Used for nausea, pain, and stress relief.

name: Reiki
note: Hands-on or hands-near energy healing modality from Japan. Used for relaxation, stress reduction, and emotional processing. Considered complementary to conventional medical care.

name: Homeopathy
note: System of medicine using highly diluted substances to stimulate the body's healing response. Used as complementary care. Consult with a trained classical homeopath for individualized prescribing.

name: Naturopathic Consultation
note: Holistic medical consultation with a licensed naturopathic doctor. Emphasizes root-cause medicine, nutrition, botanical medicine, and lifestyle interventions alongside conventional diagnostics.

name: Functional Medicine Consultation
note: Extended medical consultation focused on identifying and addressing root causes of chronic disease. Integrates advanced lab testing, lifestyle medicine, nutrition, and targeted supplementation.

name: Integrative Medicine Consultation
note: Medical consultation blending conventional and evidence-based complementary approaches. Addresses physical, emotional, and lifestyle factors in a whole-person framework.

name: Ayurvedic Medicine
note: Traditional Indian system of medicine balancing doshas (Vata, Pitta, Kapha) through diet, herbs, lifestyle, and cleansing practices. Panchakarma is the intensive purification protocol.

name: Traditional Chinese Medicine
note: Comprehensive system including acupuncture, herbal medicine, cupping, gua sha, and dietary therapy. Aims to balance qi, yin/yang, and organ systems. Used for a wide range of conditions.

name: Psychotherapy
note: Structured therapeutic conversation with a licensed mental health professional to address mental health, emotional patterns, trauma, and behavioral health. Foundation of mental health care.

name: CBT
note: Evidence-based psychotherapy that identifies and reframes maladaptive thought patterns and behaviors. First-line treatment for anxiety, depression, OCD, and insomnia (CBT-I).

name: EMDR
note: Evidence-based trauma therapy using bilateral stimulation (eye movements, tapping) to process traumatic memories. Highly effective for PTSD and complex trauma. Typically 6-12 sessions.

name: Somatic Therapy
note: Body-centered psychotherapy that works with physical sensations to process and release trauma stored in the nervous system. Includes somatic experiencing, sensorimotor psychotherapy.

name: EFT (Emotional Freedom Technique)
note: Self-administered or practitioner-guided technique tapping acupressure points while focusing on emotional distress. Used for anxiety, phobias, PTSD, and pain. Easy to learn for self-care.

name: MBSR
note: Structured 8-week group program combining mindfulness meditation, body scan, and yoga. Clinically validated for chronic pain, anxiety, depression, and stress-related conditions.

name: Biofeedback
note: Uses sensors to provide real-time physiological data (heart rate, muscle tension, skin conductance) to teach voluntary control of body functions. Used for anxiety, headaches, and pelvic floor.

name: Neurofeedback
note: Real-time EEG feedback to train brainwave patterns. Used for ADHD, anxiety, PTSD, insomnia, and traumatic brain injury. Requires multiple sessions for durable results.

name: Hypnotherapy
note: Guided hypnotic state to access subconscious patterns for behavior change, pain management, anxiety, IBS, and phobias. Gut-directed hypnotherapy is evidence-based for IBS.

name: Grief Counseling
note: Specialized therapy for processing grief, loss, and bereavement. Addresses complicated grief, trauma-related loss, and anticipatory grief. Individual or group formats available.

name: Couples Therapy
note: Psychotherapy for couples addressing communication, conflict, intimacy, and relationship challenges. Evidence-based modalities include Gottman Method, EFT for couples, and Imago therapy.

name: Endocrinologist
note: Medical specialist for endocrine system disorders including thyroid disease, diabetes, adrenal conditions, PCOS, pituitary disorders, and hormone imbalances.

name: Rheumatologist
note: Specialist for autoimmune and inflammatory conditions including rheumatoid arthritis, lupus, Sjogren's, ankylosing spondylitis, and vasculitis.

name: Gastroenterologist
note: Specialist for digestive system disorders including IBD, IBS, SIBO, celiac disease, liver disease, and gastrointestinal motility disorders. Performs endoscopy and colonoscopy.

name: Neurologist
note: Specialist for brain, spinal cord, and peripheral nervous system conditions including migraine, epilepsy, MS, neuropathy, movement disorders, and cognitive impairment.

name: Cardiologist
note: Specialist for cardiovascular conditions including coronary artery disease, arrhythmia, heart failure, and preventive cardiology. Integrative cardiologists also address lifestyle and inflammation.

name: Dermatologist
note: Specialist for skin, hair, and nail conditions including acne, eczema, psoriasis, rosacea, dermatitis, and skin cancer screening. Integrative dermatology addresses internal root causes.

name: Gynecologist
note: Specialist in female reproductive health including menstrual disorders, PCOS, fibroids, endometriosis, pelvic floor issues, menopause, and fertility. Annual well-woman exam recommended.

name: Urologist
note: Specialist for urinary tract and male reproductive health including interstitial cystitis, kidney stones, prostate health, UTIs, and sexual dysfunction.

name: Allergist
note: Specialist for allergic conditions including seasonal allergies, food allergies, eczema, urticaria, angioedema, anaphylaxis, and MCAS. Performs allergy testing and oversees immunotherapy.

name: Immunologist
note: Specialist for immune system dysfunction including immune deficiency, autoimmune disease, MCAS, and recurrent infections. Often same specialist as allergist (allergist-immunologist).

name: Functional Medicine Doctor
note: Physician trained in functional medicine root-cause approach. Extended consultation time, advanced laboratory testing, and personalized lifestyle and therapeutic protocols.

name: Pelvic Health Specialist
note: Specialist in conditions affecting the pelvic floor and pelvic organs including incontinence, prolapse, pelvic pain, and sexual dysfunction. May be a urogynecologist or PT.

name: Naturopathic Doctor
note: Licensed naturopathic physician using nutrition, botanical medicine, homeopathy, physical medicine, and lifestyle counseling. Prescribing scope varies by state or province.

name: Oil Pulling
note: Swish 1 tablespoon of coconut or sesame oil in mouth for 10-20 minutes, then spit out. Morning practice before eating. Supports oral microbiome, reduces plaque, and may reduce bacteria.

name: Dry Brushing
note: Brush dry skin in long strokes toward the heart before showering. Supports lymphatic flow, exfoliates dead skin cells, and may improve circulation. Use natural bristle brush.

name: Epsom Salt Bath
note: Dissolve 1-2 cups Epsom salts (magnesium sulfate) in warm bath. Used for muscle relaxation, stress reduction, and sleep preparation. Transdermal magnesium absorption debated but widely used.

name: Contrast Shower
note: Alternating hot (2-3 minutes) and cold (30-60 seconds) water cycles. Stimulates circulation, lymphatic flow, and vagal tone. End on cold for stimulation or warm for relaxation.

name: Infrared Sauna
note: Low-temperature (110-140°F) infrared heat session lasting 20-45 minutes. Promotes sweating, cardiovascular conditioning, detoxification, pain relief, and parasympathetic recovery.

name: Cold Plunge
note: Immersion in cold water (45-59°F) for 1-10 minutes. Activates brown fat, reduces inflammation, boosts norepinephrine, and supports mental resilience. Do not do immediately after strength training.

name: Red Light Therapy
note: Application of red (630-700nm) and near-infrared (800-1100nm) light to tissue. Supports mitochondrial function, wound healing, hair growth, pain reduction, and skin rejuvenation.

name: Grounding
note: Direct skin contact with the earth (grass, soil, sand, water). Proposed to transfer electrons and reduce chronic inflammation, improve sleep, and reduce cortisol. 20-30 minutes minimum.

name: Float Therapy
note: Floating in a dark, soundproof tank of dense Epsom salt solution at body temperature. Deeply reduces sensory input and activates the parasympathetic nervous system. Reduces cortisol, anxiety, and pain.

name: Salt Room (Halotherapy)
note: Inhalation of dry pharmaceutical-grade salt aerosol in a controlled environment. Used for respiratory conditions (asthma, bronchitis, COPD), skin conditions, and stress relief.

name: IV Vitamin C
note: High-dose intravenous ascorbic acid (10-50g+) bypassing GI absorption limits. Used for immune support, cancer adjunct therapy, viral illness, and Lyme disease. Requires G6PD screening.

name: IV Myers Cocktail
note: Intravenous blend of magnesium, calcium, B vitamins, and vitamin C. Used for fatigue, fibromyalgia, migraines, immune support, and acute illness recovery.

name: IV Glutathione
note: Intravenous administration of the master antioxidant. Supports detoxification, neurological conditions, skin brightening, and immune function. Often given after other IV infusions.

name: IV NAD+
note: Intravenous nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide. Used for addiction recovery, fatigue, cognitive optimization, and cellular repair. Infusions take 4-8 hours; may cause discomfort during infusion.

name: B12 Injection
note: Intramuscular or subcutaneous B12 injection bypassing GI absorption issues. Used for deficiency, pernicious anemia, fatigue, neurological support, and poor oral absorption.

name: Iron IV Infusion
note: Intravenous iron repletion for significant iron deficiency when oral supplementation is insufficient, intolerable, or poorly absorbed. Formulations include ferric carboxymaltose and iron sucrose.

name: PRP Injection
note: Injection of concentrated autologous platelets to stimulate tissue repair. Used for tendon injuries, osteoarthritis, and hair restoration. Evidence strongest for knee OA and chronic tendinopathy.

name: Prolotherapy
note: Injection of irritant solution (typically dextrose) into ligaments or tendons to stimulate healing of chronic injuries. Used for joint instability, chronic tendinopathy, and back pain.

name: Ozone Therapy
note: Medical application of ozone-oxygen mixture via injection, autohemotherapy, or rectal insufflation. Used for infections, chronic disease, and immune modulation. Requires trained practitioner.

name: Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy
note: Breathing 100% oxygen under elevated atmospheric pressure (1.5-3 ATA). Promotes tissue healing, neuroplasticity, and infection resolution. Studied for TBI, post-COVID, wound healing, and Lyme.

name: Yoga
note: Mind-body practice combining movement, breath, and meditation. Supports flexibility, strength, stress reduction, nervous system regulation, and mental health. Adaptable for all fitness levels.

name: Restorative Yoga
note: Passive, supported poses held for several minutes to activate the parasympathetic nervous system. Used for recovery, adrenal fatigue, chronic illness, and stress reduction.

name: Pilates
note: Core-centered movement practice for posture, strength, and mobility. Clinical pilates adapts for injury rehab and postpartum recovery. Reformer pilates provides additional resistance.

name: Tai Chi
note: Slow, flowing movement meditation with roots in Chinese martial arts. Evidence-based for balance improvement, fall prevention, blood pressure reduction, and arthritis management.

name: Qigong
note: Ancient Chinese movement and breathing practice cultivating qi (vital energy). Supports stress reduction, immune function, cardiovascular health, and lymphatic flow. Low impact and accessible.

name: Dance Therapy
note: Psychotherapeutic use of movement and dance to support emotional, cognitive, and social integration. Used for trauma, depression, Parkinson's disease, and communication disorders.
